authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinics (CCBHCs)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA defines the core community behavioral health model, required service categories, and terminology used across community mental health delivery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service-model framing and community mental health terminology normalization
used_for: person- and family-centered treatment planning terminology

authoritative_sources.title: QSO-20-05 CMHC: Revised State Operations Manual Chapter 2 and Appendix F for Community Mental Health Centers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS establishes authoritative community mental health center requirements for initial evaluation, comprehensive assessment, active treatment plan, discharge, and quality improvement evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accepted case execution, documentation control, discharge, and quality review

authoritative_sources.title: 2025 National Guidelines for a Behavioral Health Coordinated System of Crisis Care
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's national crisis guidance defines when routine community mental health work must convert into crisis escalation, warm handoff, safety planning, and follow-up.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Crisis interruption, safety escalation, and post-crisis follow-up

authoritative_sources.title: Information Related to Mental and Behavioral Health, including Opioid Overdose
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OCR is the HIPAA enforcement authority and provides the operative community behavioral health disclosure boundaries for treatment, family involvement, and health-and-safety sharing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy-bound communication, family requests, and health-and-safety disclosure control

authoritative_sources.title: Individuals' Right under HIPAA to Access their Health Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The HIPAA access guidance defines the designated record set and timing obligations that constrain what counts as auditable completion and lawful record handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record custody, completion evidence, and client-access handling

authoritative_sources.title: Understanding Confidentiality of Substance Use Disorder (SUD) Patient Records or Part 2
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS states the current Part 2 baseline, 2024 final-rule updates, and restrictions on legal-proceeding use that materially affect mixed mental health and SUD records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated disclosure control for mixed mental health and SUD records

authoritative_sources.title: CCBHC Quality Measures Guidance and Webinars
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA ties community behavioral health operations to named access, follow-up, and quality-measure evidence expectations rather than generic completion claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Completion evidence, follow-up tracking, and audit readiness

workflow_stages.name: Accepted episode setup
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the accepted community mental health episode is tied to referral or admission context, current assessment, and an assigned treatment-team or care-coordination owner before routine execution proceeds.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Active treatment-plan execution
workflow_stages.description: Advance work only when the person-centered treatment plan, encounter schedule, consent posture, and assigned coordination record are current and attributable.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Crisis interruption and safety routing
workflow_stages.description: Stop routine workflow when facts indicate crisis, self-harm, or other safety-critical escalation and shift to crisis or licensed-clinical ownership with a documented handoff.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure and record-control checks
workflow_stages.description: Apply HIPAA, representative-status, ROI, and Part 2 constraints before sharing treatment information or counting a record action as complete.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Discharge, transfer, and completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Require progress-note, follow-up, transfer, and discharge evidence in the designated source-of-truth record before marking work complete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Person-centered treatment plan and treatment-plan review history
Encounter schedule, attendance, and progress notes
Crisis screening, crisis or safety plan, and crisis follow-up tasks
Consent, release-of-information, guardian, and representative records
Care coordination notes and external referral tracking
Hospital or crisis transition note and discharge or transfer summary
Quality-measure evidence and audit trail records
systems_of_record: Behavioral health EHR or clinical record system
Case management or care coordination platform
Scheduling, attendance, and encounter documentation system
Consent, ROI, and privacy management repository
Crisis line, mobile crisis, or emergency handoff log
Quality reporting, audit, and supervisory review system
Tenant policy repository for program, county, payer, and licensure overlays

allowed: Advance, hold, rework, or complete community mental health workflow steps when required records and tenant context are present.
Prepare evidence-backed execution summaries, exception notes, completion records, and next-step packages.
Map tenant terminology to public community mental health baseline artifacts when the mapping is explicit and auditable.
escalate: Crisis, self-harm, threat, abuse, or other safety-critical facts requiring licensed-clinical or emergency response ownership
Privacy, ROI, representative-status, or Part 2 uncertainty that changes whether information can be shared or work can proceed
Conflicting public and tenant policy on documentation cadence, closure requirements, or disclosure handling
Missing transition, discharge, or crisis handoff documentation that prevents safe follow-up
refuse: Requests to diagnose, prescribe, change medications, revise treatment modality, or determine involuntary treatment disposition
Requests to treat a generic ticketing record as sufficient evidence for behavioral health completion
Requests to disclose protected mental health or SUD information without valid authority or traceable supporting records
Requests to close work without progress-note, treatment-plan, discharge, or equivalent source-of-truth evidence

domain_failure_modes: Progressing a community mental health episode without a current assessment or active treatment plan.
Using generic customer support records instead of the designated behavioral health record to justify closure.
Treating crisis or safety facts as routine follow-up instead of switching to crisis escalation and handoff.
Sharing mental health or SUD treatment information without valid ROI, representative authority, or Part 2 handling.
Closing a case without discharge, transfer, or progress documentation tied to the source system.
Drifting into licensed-clinical, legal, or payer authority because the community mental health boundary was underspecified.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local program model differences across CCBHC, CMHC, and county-operated community clinics
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Specific artifact names, staffing roles, and completion checkpoints vary by tenant and jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of the active program model and preserve the public baseline when mapping local artifacts.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific minor-consent, guardian, and representative access rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Disclosure and family-communication decisions can change materially by jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Block or escalate when state-specific authority is not retrieved instead of applying generic HIPAA assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific documentation cadence and signoff rules for discharge or transfer
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baseline establishes the need for auditable completion evidence, but the exact cadence and approval chain can vary.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant policy retrieval before final closure and record the governing local rule in the execution summary.

notes: Research is validated because workflow, evidence, terminology, privacy, and failure-handling sources are all covered by authoritative public references.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; all remaining ambiguity items are explicit retrieval dependencies or escalation triggers.
The lane is materially different from generic operations because community mental health records, crisis routing, and disclosure controls determine whether work can continue.
